AVF17Rfh0m6YI: Our goal is a safe, welcoming, and creative space. These Guidelines explain how to behave on Clovord and what content is allowed. They complement our
iV7RlGr2r8: The Guidelines are a practical rulebook for day-to-day use. They are not a legal contract—that’s the
gxlqpCpjf: —but violations can lead to enforcement actions under the ToS.
7dvuBLyMKPw: Treat others with dignity. Disagree without personal attacks. Create inclusive spaces.
rTwUBsbWyoG: Do not endanger others. No incitement to violence, doxxing, or encouragement of self-harm.
Kjp6ZkmP: Share helpful, creative, or constructive content. Keep discussions on-topic in each channel.
evMEEE2pqIX: Respect local laws, these Guidelines, server rules, and moderator instructions.
lYBA7zr68P: No harassment, threats, hate speech, or targeted insults against protected characteristics.
ij7H2pZ9: No bullying, stalking, or coordinated harassment campaigns.
hj6SJs6A22: No exploitation or endangerment of minors. Zero tolerance.
TfXCxqUAwmD: No sharing of others’ private information (doxxing) without explicit consent.
wyD0wXzb: No glorification of self-harm, suicide instructions, or encouragement of dangerous activities.
aZeLAf9r2LME: Respect channel topics, server rules, and staff instructions.
1F9OgLbBb98h: Illegal content; extremist propaganda; terrorist recruitment or praise.
wTGt7oUSDt4f: Sexual content involving minors; sexualization of minors (including drawings/AI); revenge porn.
0UBxXllV6s: Malware, phishing kits, credential theft, or instructions to bypass security.
G5iBs8DZs: Graphic violence or gore shared to shock or harm others.
ZCzZApCrnbud: IP infringement (piracy, cracked software, stolen assets) and privacy violations.
Fdlr2xXvX: Educational/security research must be clearly contextualized and lawful; never distribute active payloads.
JJGOznaK: Medical, legal, or financial advice must include appropriate disclaimers when relevant.
Mq0QYeVEX: Servers/channels hosting adult content must be clearly labeled and access-restricted.
NK0m4eRzizFr: No sexual content involving minors—directly or indirectly. Zero tolerance.
eQJuGwN45J: Do not use unsolicited NSFW avatars, banners, or usernames in general spaces.
sKJCqADPyWHTM: Graphic violence/gore must be avoided in general spaces; use clear warnings and only if contextually necessary.
zLt1XtN8s: No unsolicited mass DMs, link spam, or deceptive redirects.
q6Du6PGSjUSU: No scams, impersonation of staff, or fake “support” accounts.
6CQaUVsFp5: Bots and automation must follow rate limits and provide opt-out where applicable.
wlRiRC4U41D7: No artificial engagement (fake likes, boosted numbers) to manipulate discovery.
bf4fpykVK: No hateful slurs, explicit sexual content, or graphic violence in names/avatars/banners.
YhqBcElM4siY: No impersonation of individuals, brands, or Clovord staff. Parody must be clear.
TNyhfrnnakctl: Respect trademark and copyright when using logos or brand names.
ZYvYLEQVI: Follow each server’s rules for promos, invites, or affiliate links.
pkRthQGGnL: Disclose paid promotions or sponsorships clearly.
WraIgHpJg: No misleading claims or fake giveaways.
uYngdxBGD7k: Servers must maintain clear local rules consistent with these Guidelines and the ToS.
xJOsEtBv8: Moderators should act consistently, document major actions, and avoid conflicts of interest.
6nCl9hnB: Use channel topics, access controls, and slowmode to maintain order where needed.
s3UxJn0VPTmt: Immediately remove illegal content and report it where required by law.
8V6T9nZzwV: Enforcement actions depend on context and severity and may include:
LCin3Z6GbbZOc: Content removal, warnings, temporary mutes or feature restrictions.
oEOAtthLZRwmlV: Timed suspensions, server-level bans, or platform account termination.
cLEAioQUcB: Reporting to authorities when legally required or in cases of imminent harm.
SkOheZHWDxAxO: We consider intent, harm, history of behavior, and cooperation when deciding outcomes.
nQldS1cLhrB8: Report violations via in-app tools or your server’s moderators.
uUrdZmjlC1Po1: Appeals should be concise, factual, and include relevant message links/IDs.
07eouiLe2u8RTk: Submitting repeated or abusive appeals may lead to restrictions.

6WgR1fnntInN: Schedule events, RSVP in one tap, and record highlights. Time zones handled. Reminders built‑in.

6AGrgm5zOsBJ1e: Welcome to the Clovord Reference! Here you'll find all the information you need to integrate with our API. Clovord provides powerful tools to interact with your data, manage resources, and automate workflows.
dsR9pPtmvXWpd5: All API requests are made to the following base URL:
hjD7bgd2Cg: Clovord uses versioning to ensure backward compatibility and to allow for continuous improvements. Each version is identified by a version number in the URL path, such as ``https://clovord.com/api/v{version_number}``. When breaking changes are introduced, a new major version is released (e.g., `v2`). Minor updates that are backward-compatible are added to the current version.
8x8nqMItgY: Clovord uses a consistent error response format to help developers debug and resolve issues efficiently. Below is an overview of common error codes and their meanings.
CxFmjzZ4wx7X: The request was invalid or cannot be served. Check the request parameters or body.
Efv8ItIJAFDB2K: Authentication is required and has failed or has not been provided.
2hnjXjFIbpb4iB: The request is understood, but it has been refused or access is not allowed.
SsYm3RYeLwM4q: The requested resource could not be found.
lRlBZOd1hT7ASK: The resource that is being accessed is locked by the Experiments System.
Pfm0Q5Z3ZpDLK: The request is missing important data that result in a block from the firewall.
XxOiU2XmrGgs: The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time ("rate limiting").
4enhoBGz0nFf: The requesters IP/Account got banned/blocked from the firewall.
V11FjjX4: An unexpected error occurred on the server that you should report immediately
dB0QwPqTYHkeG: The server is currently unavailable (e.g., due to maintenance or overload), check the statuspage then.
HAdoz26Ox9MRN: Every failure now returns a single
PLJ3R89cS: values are defined centrally so client SDKs can rely on a stable registry.
GMlG8EE35Zo: Stable numeric identifier from the Clovord registry (e.g.,
CPKMynVBIuGDrI: Human friendly summary from the registry. Overrideable per endpoint when needed.
i39ahSIDxfbZ: Structured metadata for clients. Validation errors mirror our
UQg6kTF2Gscja: if provided, otherwise generated) for correlation.
Kd4GNBNLZZTDWP: UTC timestamp generated when the error left the API.
SGGeAFbJ: Validation failures keep the same envelope but populate
y3gNexSTbg4: blocks so you can map issues back to form inputs:
RNDzBn4B31: { "error": { "code": 50034, "message": "Invalid Form Body", "details": { "body": { "_errors": [ { "code": "VALUE_ERROR.MISSING", "message": "Field 'name' is required" } ] }, "email": { "_errors": [ { "code": "VALUE_ERROR.EMAIL", "message": "Email address is invalid" } ] } }, "request_id": "b4157c52-6125-4a5d-a363-1cf53d1ad3f5", "ts": "2025-11-14T12:35:01.004Z" } }
AlMkfnVOe: Clovord uses token-based authentication to ensure secure access to its endpoints. All API requests must include an
CRF4KcOS8G: Use the format "Bot {token}" for the "Authorization" header.
WJXmAQMg8kv: Replace "YOUR_TOKEN_HERE" with your actual API token. If your token is invalid or missing, the API will return a "401 Unauthorized" error.
70Wg22wGry: The token is missing, invalid, or expired.
OjHt6HGdzw: The token does not have permission to access the requested resource.
V7ZkI75pENyVEc: Clovord uses the UNIX timestamp format for representing date and time values. This ensures simplicity and compatibility across different systems.
0XKQEPNv: All timestamps are represented as the number of seconds since "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z" (UTC).
Ma6Eyfy0qJ997: The creation timestamp of a resource.
IiWbaLdqXbTJLu: The last update timestamp of a resource.
9GKeo2c8bvM: The expiration timestamp of a resource.
2ezpqOvB: If a timestamp is invalid or not in the correct format, the API will return a "400 Bad Request" error.
pQI3tA1HGcJh4: Clovord uses a consistent convention for nullable and optional fields. Resource fields that may contain a null value have types that are prefixed with a question mark. Resource fields that are optional have names that are suffixed with a question mark.
ammhEibskzzSZD: Fields marked with "?"" are optional and can be omitted from the request. If omitted, the API will use a default value or ignore the field.
wALA7CTId: Examples of Nullable and Optional Fields
2VVRhxvAhpDmo: If a required field is missing, or if a nullable field contains an invalid value, the API will return a "400 Bad Request" error.
qRy2o97B: Clovord follows RESTful principles and uses standard HTTP methods for interacting with resources. Below are the key requirements and limitations for using the API.
7SofeyJuL: All requests must include a valid "User-Agent" header that identifies your application or integration. Requests without a valid "User-Agent" may be rejected for security reasons.
31VHMM9au: For endpoints that accept a request body, the "Content-Type" header must be set to "application/json". Requests with an invalid or missing "Content-Type" header will result in a "400 Bad Request" error.
UILyO9lSkc: Clovord enforces rate limits to ensure fair usage and prevent abuse. If you exceed the allowed number of requests, the API will return a "429 Too Many Requests" error. Use the "X-RateLimit-Reset" header in the response to determine when you can resume sending requests.

y2x8LqZpVf: Below are the structures and objects used in the Domain category.
k9BvQ2mLxR: The deterministic hash of the normalized domain
F7pLx3mKqD: The domain name in normalized form
n8rTQ1xLzV: Comma-separated tags attached to the domain
V4mP2xRtQa: Optional reason for the domain entry
m7Qw8LrPz1: Additional metadata for the domain entry
b2VxQ7mLp9: Timestamp of when the domain was added
P3kLm7xQdV: Threat confidence score from 0 to 100; higher values indicate greater certainty
B9xQm4rLzW: Bitmask integer encoding domain threat and trust properties. See the Domain Flags section for details.
u1QmV7xLp3: Returns a full list of Domain Object entries.
z7QpL2mVx6: Returns a list of of Domain Object entries matching the query
v2QxLm8pR5: Update a domain entry by hash or domain.
W6xQm2LpV9: Delete a domain entry by hash or domain.

w1QmVx9Lp3: Filter by tag or metadata status string.
V5pQm2LxR8: Max number of items to return (1-200).
R2mQx8LpV1: Number of items to skip before returning results.
s6QmV1LpX8: Tags for the entry (array or comma-separated).
k2QmVx9Lp1: Hash of the domain entry to update.
n5QmLp7Vx3: Hash of the domain entry to delete.

pR4xVn9Lm2Qd: Used for API responses that return per-item statuses in items[]
C3pLx8Vq1NmR: Request metadata (id, timestamp, path, method, encrypted)
R7vQm2Lp6XkN: Top-level status block with http.code, code, and code_message
Y4mLp9Qx2VtB: Present for standard success responses when top-level code is not 207*
B1xQv7Lm5RpC: Present for standard error responses when top-level code is not 207*
N9pVm3Lx7QdK: Present only for top-level 207* responses (success and error multi-status)
U3vQm9Lp1XkT: clovord_response: when top-level code starts with 207, the HTTP status is always 207 and the payload must be sent via items[]
D8mLp4Qv7XnC: clovord_error: multi-status is supported only when top-level code starts with 207; error items are returned in items[]
A5qVx1Lm9RpN: payload is not supported for 207* responses/errors and items is only supported for 207*
J7vLp2Qm6XnD: Top-level code that starts with 207 uses Multi-Status envelope (items[], HTTP 207)
F4xQm9Lp1VtR: Top-level codes not starting with 207 use standard envelope (data for success, errors for errors)
